Event: Sandy easily defeats SpongeBob in kung-fu battles
Explanation: She practices and proves her strength, while SpongeBob is repeatedly beaten up for her benefit/fun.

Event: Sandy and SpongeBob initially bond over karate
Explanation: Shared karate practice builds mutual skills and deepens their friendship.

Event: Sandy defends SpongeBob from terror
Explanation: She protects him from threats, helping him while reinforcing her protector role and bonds.

Event: Sandy stands up for SpongeBob and Patrick when they are bullied
Explanation: She confronts bullies to shield her friends, benefiting them and enhancing her own standing.

Event: Sandy resigns from Mr. Krabs and removes her barbeque nuts from the menu in response to his treatment of SpongeBob
Explanation: She sacrifices a job but asserts her values and denies Krabs profit, while incidentally removing a product customers might want.

Event: Sandy coerces Squidward to join Patrick’s game and shuts him up when he mocks it
Explanation: She forces him to participate constructively, curbing his mockery and improving the group’s experience.

Event: Sandy goes to take out the Alaskan Bullworm for the sake of the town, ignoring SpongeBob’s warnings
Explanation: Her reckless overconfidence endangers herself and fails to help the town at that stage.

Event: Sandy fights what she thinks is the Alaskan Bullworm in the cave and defeats it
Explanation: She proves her toughness to herself but actually only attacks the tongue, briefly harming the creature without solving the real threat.

Event: Sandy runs for her life after realizing she fought only the worm’s tongue
Explanation: She saves herself by fleeing but abandons the bravado goal and offers no help to others in danger.

Event: Sandy challenges massive beachgoers to weightlifting contests
Explanation: She hones her strength and competitive drive while giving others a fair physical challenge.

Event: Mr. Krabs adds Sandy’s barbeque nuts to the menu and coldly dismisses SpongeBob and Krabby Patties
Explanation: He boosts his business/profits but harms SpongeBob emotionally and professionally.

Event: Sandy takes SpongeBob along for extreme stunts in “Prehibernation Week” despite his deteriorating health
Explanation: She gets intense training and fun while severely endangering and exhausting SpongeBob.

Event: Sandy rallies the entire town to search for SpongeBob in dangerous places, ignoring their injuries
Explanation: Her concern for SpongeBob drives her, but she recklessly harms the townsfolk’s safety and comfort for her goal.

Event: Sandy destroys the city when everyone bails on the search
Explanation: She vents frustration by wrecking the city, harming everyone and leaving herself in a ruined environment with no gain.

Event: Sandy tells SpongeBob he shouldn’t be rude while having earlier taken advantage of him
Explanation: She asserts a moral stance that may help SpongeBob but exposes her hypocrisy and undermines her credibility.

Event: Sandy exposes Kenny the Cat as a fraud in front of his fans and kicks him back to the surface without provocation
Explanation: She satisfies her animal pride and “truth-telling” urge while humiliating and ejecting him, helping no one else.

Event: Sandy mistakes cats and dogs regarding a squirrel’s enemy in “Kenny the Cat”
Explanation: Her ignorance harms her own credibility while not clearly affecting others.

Event: Sandy invokes the “Idiot Ball” on herself in “Squirrel Jokes” to show SpongeBob how toxic a bigoted friend is
Explanation: She makes herself the target of hurtful behavior to teach him a lesson, sacrificing her own comfort for his growth.

Event: Sandy feeds freeloaders toxic waste out of curiosity in “SpongeBob, You’re Fired”
Explanation: Her reckless experiment endangers others’ health and offers her no real benefit beyond idle curiosity.

Event: Sandy expresses annoyance at Patrick’s stupidity and tells him to “go be stupid somewhere else”
Explanation: She relieves her own irritation while dismissing him harshly, slightly harming his feelings without much upside for him.

Event: Sandy forces SpongeBob to join extreme sports in “Prehibernation Week” while oblivious to his despair
Explanation: She gets training and enjoyment while inflicting serious fear and physical strain on him.

Event: Sandy refuses to let the searchers sleep while looking for SpongeBob during “Prehibernation Week”
Explanation: Her obsessive concern for SpongeBob drives her, but she exhausts and endangers the townsfolk.

Event: Sandy repeatedly brags that land creatures are better than sea creatures in “Pressure”
Explanation: Her ego gets stroked while she disrespects others and contributes to a dangerous rivalry.

Event: Sandy nearly drowns herself trying to prove land creatures are better in “Pressure”
Explanation: Her pride nearly kills her and offers no real benefit, slightly prompting others to re-examine their stance.

Event: Sandy grows out of her land-vs-sea superiority after the mutual danger in “Pressure”
Explanation: She learns from the near-disaster, dropping her bigotry and improving relationships and safety.

Event: Sandy mocks SpongeBob for being afraid when investigating roaring noises in “Scaredy Snail”
Explanation: She bolsters her image as brave at the cost of belittling his fear.

Event: Sandy secretly banks on SpongeBob backing out so she doesn’t have to enter the trench in “Scaredy Snail”
Explanation: Her cowardly dependence gives her an excuse not to go but undermines her own self-image and contributes no help to him.

Event: Sandy runs for her life when an actual monster appears in “Scaredy Snail”
Explanation: She sensibly preserves herself but abandons her earlier “tough” pose and leaves others to face the threat.

Event: Sandy rallies citizens to look for SpongeBob out of concern when she finds his pants caught on coral
Explanation: Her genuine worry mobilizes a community rescue effort that, at least initially, aims to protect him and gives others a chance to help.

Event: Sandy makes everyone search in increasingly dangerous places as time goes on in “Prehibernation Week”
Explanation: Her fixation on saving SpongeBob leads her to risk others’ safety far beyond reason.

Event: Sandy proceeds to destroy the city after the townsfolk refuse to keep helping her search
Explanation: She lashes out by wrecking property, hurting everyone and leaving herself in a devastated town.
